method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/bsa (Akamai + nginx + AWS ALB + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform v22 + Dynatrace). Confirmed there is NO online application portal: intake is downloadable PDF forms (bz_form.pdf, appeal_form.pdf, bzy_form.pdf, soc_form.pdf) filed on paper, and the resolutions/records search is a server-rendered Livesite widget (searchRecords(), submit=true&componentID=…) with no JSON/OpenAPI. Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Board of Standards and Appeals (BSA)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 4 assets with column schemas; sampled live SODA data to enumerate application types (BZ/BZY/SOC/Appeal) and statuses (Granted/Denied/Withdrawn/Dismissed).

meta.platform: NYC.gov 'Livesite' (Akamai + nginx + AWS ALB + Dynatrace); records/decisions search is a server-rendered Livesite widget; intake is paper PDF forms — no online application portal
meta.netnew: VarianceApplication — file a zoning variance / special permit / extension / appeal (today a paper PDF-form process)
meta.tagline: BSA publishes what it decided but offers no way to file — the intake is still paper PDF forms.
meta.headline: Case outcomes are open on NYC Open Data (4 datasets), but filing a variance or appeal has no online portal and no API — digitize the intake.

scorecard_notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis; no BSA-specific platform; intake is paper PDF forms.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No site/records API — resolutions search is a server-rendered Livesite widget, no JSON/OpenAPI.
scorecard_notes.openData: 4 Socrata datasets covering case status, decisions map, legacy index, pre-app meetings; no filing/hearing data.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Core Application/case object well modeled in Open Data; resolutions only as PDF links; no hearing calendar.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write surface at all — filing a variance/appeal is a download-a-PDF-and-file-on-paper process.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP; search is not machine-callable.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Case data on Socrata/Tyler; site on shared Livesite; only the PDF forms are BSA's own — mixed, vendor-hosted.

headline_findings: BSA is a records-forward agency on the shared NYC.gov 'Livesite' chassis (Akamai/nginx/AWS ALB/Dynatrace) with NO online application portal.

Case OUTCOMES are open: 4 NYC Open Data datasets cover every application filed 1998–present with type (BZ variance/special-permit, BZY extension, SOC amendment, Appeal), premises, zoning district, status (Granted/Denied/Withdrawn/Dismissed), and a link to the decision PDF, plus a legacy calendar index back to 1916 and a pre-application-meetings log.
But the INTAKE has no machine-readable surface: filing a variance/appeal is a paper PDF-form process (bz_form.pdf, appeal_form.pdf, bzy_form.pdf, soc_form.pdf) with no online portal and no API.

The records/decisions search is a server-rendered Livesite widget (searchRecords()), not an API; resolutions are unstructured PDFs; the public hearing calendar exists only as HTML/PDF with Zoom links, with no Open Data twin.
Net-new write surface: file a zoning variance/appeal (VarianceApplication) — the core BSA transaction, with no API today. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 4 BSA datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open BSA API — case outcomes/index only.
server-rendered search widget over decided cases 1998–present (searchRecords(), submit=true&componentID=…); no JSON/OpenAPI.
bz_form.pdf, appeal_form.pdf, bzy_form.pdf, soc_form.pdf + checklists — the filing workflow, on paper. No online portal.
the written resolution per case — a PDF, not structured data.
content only; Akamai edge, nginx, AWS ALB, Dynatrace RUM. No content API.
referenced applications/zoning-lot map; a DCP surface, not a BSA API.

fruit.name: Applications (cases) status
fruit.notes: Core case object. Keyed on calendar number; type (BZ/BZY/SOC/Appeal), ZR/GCL section, premises, block/lot, BBL/BIN, zoning district, project description, status, decision date, decision PDF link.

fruit.name: Zoning lot / property BSA history
fruit.notes: Property/zoning-lot spine keyed on BBL; legacy calendar index (C1-C2-C3 = number-year-type) reaching back to 1916, with premises and geography.

fruit.name: Pre-application meetings
fruit.notes: Pre-filing consultation log — applicant, meeting date, application type, premises, geography.

fruit.name: Resolutions (decisions)
fruit.notes: Each decision is a linked PDF; reasoning, conditions, and vote are unstructured. No structured resolution resource today.

fruit.name: Public hearing calendar
fruit.notes: Upcoming hearings published only as HTML/PDF with Zoom webinar links; no Open Data twin. A core read surface with no dataset.

fruit.name: File a variance / special permit
fruit.notes: Filing a BZ variance / special permit — download bz_form.pdf + checklist and file on paper. No online portal, no API. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

fruit.name: File an extension of time / vested rights (BZY)
fruit.notes: BZY extension — bzy_form.pdf, paper filing. An alternative net-new write case type.

fruit.name: File an appeal of a DOB determination
fruit.notes: Appeal — appeal_form.pdf, paper filing. The same net-new write surface, appeal case type.

fruit.name: File a Special Order Calendar (SOC) amendment
fruit.notes: SOC amendment/extension of a prior grant — soc_form.pdf, paper filing. Same net-new write surface, SOC case type.

fruit.name: Track a pending filing
fruit.notes: There is no way to track a filing before decision — no online portal exists. Only decided cases appear in Open Data. Read side of the net-new surface.
